description: 30-slide technical proposal deck: SettleMint DALP for National Bank of Egypt Digital Asset Core Infrastructure (CBE/FRA regulated, with appendix diagrams)

slides.title: Digital Asset Core Infrastructure
slides.subtitle: SettleMint Proposal for National Bank of Egypt | DALP Platform | April 2026

slides.title: Executive Summary
slides.subtitle: Why SettleMint for National Bank of Egypt

slides.title: SettleMint Is the Right Partner for NBE
slides.body:
* SettleMint's Digital Asset Lifecycle Platform (DALP) is purpose-built for regulated financial institutions operating under CBE and FRA frameworks, delivering production-grade digital asset infrastructure without bespoke development risk.
* DALP is already live with regulated banks and financial institutions across the Middle East and Africa region, supporting tokenized bonds, deposits, funds, and structured products under institutional-grade control environments.
* The platform meets every mandatory requirement in NBE's RFP: segregated multi-environment architecture, API-first enterprise integration, RBAC with maker-checker controls, configurable asset lifecycle management, and CBE-aligned regulatory evidence export.
* SettleMint's implementation model is built around NBE's operational realities: existing core banking systems, CBE supervisory obligations, FRA capital markets rules, data sovereignty requirements, and Arabic reporting needs.
* Our phased delivery approach moves NBE from controlled launch to scaled operations without platform reset, protecting the institution's investment and ensuring audit continuity from day one.

slides.title: Four Pillars of NBE Readiness
slides.item_0:
Regulatory Alignment
CBE and FRA control mapping built into the platform. AML/CFT, sanctions, data residency, and audit evidence delivered as standard configuration, not custom build.
slides.item_1:
Institutional-Grade Controls
RBAC, maker-checker, full audit logs, and configurable policy limits meet NBE's internal audit and board governance expectations without compromise.
slides.item_2:
Enterprise Integration
ISO 20022-compatible API layer connects to NBE's core banking, identity, AML, and reporting systems. No isolated digital-asset island, no reconciliation debt.
slides.item_3:
Proven Delivery Model
Phased rollout with NBE teams embedded in design authority. Fixed milestones, transparent dependencies, documented runbooks, and a clear operational handoff path.

slides.title: Mandatory Requirements Coverage
slides.body: SettleMint responds to every mandatory requirement in NATIONAL-BANK-OF-EGYPT-RFP-DIGITAL-ASSET-INFRASTRUCTURE-202603 with production-ready capability, not roadmap intent.
table_data: Segregated dev, test, UAT, DR, prod environments
RBAC, segregation of duties, maker-checker, audit logs
Configurable lifecycle states, policy controls, limits
Evidence extraction for audit and supervisory review

slides.title: Key Procurement Dimensions Addressed
slides.col1:
Control Integrity
* Full transaction provenance: who initiated, which policy applied, who approved
* Immutable audit ledger for every state change
* Maker-checker enforced at platform level, not application layer
* Complete event replay for regulatory reconstruction
* Arabic-language audit reports available on demand
slides.col2:
Enterprise Coexistence
* REST and event-based APIs align with NBE's integration standards
* ISO 20022 message format support for core banking connectivity
* No reconciliation sinkhole: all settlement events write back to CBS
* Shared identity with NBE's IAM via SAML 2.0 / OIDC
* Observability stack feeds into NBE's SIEM without middleware

slides.title: DALP Platform
slides.subtitle: Architecture, API Design, and Asset Management

slides.title: DALP Platform Overview
slides.body: DALP is the production-grade digital asset lifecycle platform for regulated institutions. Configure, issue, manage, and settle any regulated digital asset class within a single governed environment. Used by banks and capital markets firms across the Middle East, Africa, and Europe.

slides.title: Multi-Environment Architecture
slides.item1:
Development
Isolated sandbox for configuration and feature development. No production data. CBE-compliant access controls apply from day one.
slides.item2:
Test Environment
Functional and regression test bed. Mirrors production schema. Used by NBE's QA and integration teams.
slides.item3:
UAT Environment
User acceptance testing with NBE business teams. Full lifecycle execution, compliance rule validation, and reporting checks.
slides.item4:
DR Environment
Hot-standby or warm-standby in secondary data centre. RTO and RPO targets tested quarterly. CBE notification protocols integrated.
slides.item5:
Production
Live regulated environment. Change-controlled promotion only. Zero-downtime deployments via blue-green strategy.
slides.item6:
Environment Promotion
Governed promotion pipeline: Dev to Test to UAT to Production. Approval gates at each stage. Full audit trail of configuration changes.

slides.title: API-First Integration Design
slides.body: DALP exposes a versioned REST API with webhook eventing for all asset operations. Every action is API-accessible, enabling NBE's CBS, reporting, and compliance systems to consume platform events without tight coupling. OpenAPI specification and sandbox available from day one.

slides.title: RBAC, Maker-Checker, and Audit Controls
slides.item_0:
Role-Based Access Control
Granular roles across system admin, product owner, operations, compliance, auditor, and regulator viewer. Roles assigned at platform, product, and environment level. No privilege creep.
slides.item_1:
Maker-Checker Enforcement
All material actions require a second authoriser with a different role. Configurable approval chains for asset design, limit changes, and settlement operations. No single-point override.
slides.item_2:
Immutable Audit Logs
Every action recorded with actor identity, timestamp, IP, role, and policy outcome. Logs are tamper-evident and exportable in structured formats for CBE and internal audit use.
slides.item_3:
Segregation of Duties
Platform enforces SoD policies: the same user cannot design, approve, and execute an asset or transaction. SoD configuration is reviewable by NBE's internal audit team.

slides.title: Asset Lifecycle Management
slides.body: DALP's Asset Designer supports configurable lifecycle states, supply limits, transfer restrictions, maturity rules, and compliance policy assignment. Every asset class — bonds, deposits, funds, sukuk — follows a deterministic state machine visible to all authorised parties.

slides.title: Settlement and Post-Trade Processing
slides.body:
* DALP supports delivery-versus-payment (DVP) settlement with atomic execution: both the asset leg and the cash leg settle simultaneously or neither does, eliminating principal risk in institutional transactions.
* The platform integrates with NBE's core banking system to trigger debit-credit instructions at settlement completion, writing confirmed settlement events back to the CBS general ledger without manual intervention.
* Post-trade processing includes automated reconciliation against CBS positions, exception flagging with maker-checker resolution workflow, and structured settlement reports exportable in Arabic and English.
* Settlement states (pending, locked, executed, cancelled, expired) are all auditable and reconstructable. Every settlement can be replayed from the audit log for regulatory review or dispute resolution.
* NSCC and CBE domestic payment rail connectivity is supported via the API integration layer. SettleMint will work with NBE's integration team to confirm specific rail protocols during the design phase.

slides.title: Compliance and Security
slides.subtitle: Regulatory Alignment, AML/CFT, and Data Governance

slides.title: Regulatory Alignment for Egypt
slides.col1:
CBE Framework
* Central Bank of Egypt licensing requirements mapped to platform controls
* Digital asset classification support: payment, deposit analogue, security
* CBE evidence extraction: structured exports for supervisory review
* Outsourcing governance documentation aligned to CBE guidelines
slides.col2:
FRA Capital Markets
* Financial Regulatory Authority obligations for capital markets instruments
* Investor eligibility and allowlisting for regulated securities
* Disclosure and documentation controls at instrument level
* Reporting formats aligned to FRA submission requirements
slides.col3:
Islamic Finance
* Sharia governance lineage tracked: board approval, product classification, AAOIFI reference
* Sukuk and Islamic deposit product templates available
* Profit distribution and rate-reset mechanics configurable
* Documentation controls for Sharia audit trail and fatwa evidence

slides.title: AML/CFT and Sanctions Integration
slides.body: DALP's compliance engine runs pre-trade and post-trade screening against configurable watchlists including OFAC, UN Security Council, and CBE-mandated lists. Integration with NBE's existing AML platform via API ensures no duplication of screening infrastructure. Alert workflows include maker-checker resolution and full case management evidence.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/jpg/Compliance Policy Detail 1.jpg

slides.title: Data Governance and Encryption
slides.col1:
Data Residency and Sovereignty
* All NBE data hosted in Egypt-based infrastructure (on-premises or CBE-approved cloud region)
* No transaction or customer data processed outside the designated jurisdiction
* Cross-border data transfer restrictions enforced at platform layer
* Data maps and DPIA available for CBE and legal review
* Arabic character set support across all data fields and reports
slides.col2:
Retention, Encryption, and Access
* Data retained per CBE and Egyptian banking law schedules (minimum 10 years for audit records)
* AES-256 encryption at rest; TLS 1.3 in transit across all layers
* Key management via HSM with NBE-held master keys: SettleMint cannot access data without NBE authorisation
* Access logging on every data export, including regulator access and bulk extraction events
* Deletion workflows compliant with retention policy before cryptographic erasure

slides.title: Operational Resilience and DR
slides.item_0:
Recovery Objectives
RTO of 4 hours and RPO of 1 hour for production environment. DR environment maintained in secondary data centre with continuous replication. Tested quarterly with documented evidence.
slides.item_1:
Backup and Restore
Automated daily snapshots with point-in-time recovery. Backup integrity validated weekly. Restore exercises conducted quarterly with NBE operations team participation.
slides.item_2:
Incident Management
ITIL-aligned incident classification with P1 response within 30 minutes. Escalation matrix includes NBE, SettleMint, and CBE notification as required. Post-incident reports within 48 hours.
slides.item_3:
Third-Party Risk
All cloud, HSM, and managed-service dependencies disclosed in the Dependency Register (Appendix D). Each dependency has a named owner, SLA commitment, and continuity plan reviewed annually.

slides.title: Observability Stack
slides.body: DALP ships with a production-ready observability layer covering metrics, structured logs, and distributed traces. Grafana dashboards are pre-configured for asset volumes, API latency, settlement throughput, compliance alert rates, and blockchain node health. Arabic-language dashboard variants available for NBE operations teams.

slides.title: Security Architecture
slides.col1:
Identity and Access
* SAML 2.0 and OIDC federation with NBE's enterprise IAM
* Multi-factor authentication enforced for all privileged roles
* Just-in-time access provisioning for break-glass scenarios
* Session management with configurable timeout and IP binding
* Privileged access workstations for admin operations
slides.col2:
Encryption and Key Management
* AES-256 encryption at rest for all data stores
* TLS 1.3 for all data in transit, including internal service mesh
* HSM-backed key management with NBE as primary key custodian
* Key rotation schedule aligned to CBE cybersecurity guidelines
* Cryptographic evidence of key custody in audit logs
slides.col3:
Vulnerability and Penetration
* Annual third-party penetration test with findings shared with NBE CISO
* Continuous vulnerability scanning via integrated SIEM feed
* OWASP Top 10 remediation SLA: critical within 24 hours
* Secure SDLC: SAST, DAST, and dependency scanning in CI/CD pipeline
* CBE cybersecurity framework alignment documented in Security Pack

slides.title: Enterprise Integration Blueprint
slides.body: DALP integrates with NBE's enterprise stack through a combination of REST APIs, event streams, and batch interfaces. All integration points are documented in the Dependency Register and tested during WS-03.

slides.title: Reconciliation Framework
slides.body:
* DALP maintains a continuous position ledger for every digital asset and participant account, enabling real-time reconciliation against NBE's core banking system without batch dependency.
* End-of-day reconciliation reports compare DALP token positions against CBS general ledger entries. Discrepancies are automatically flagged in a structured exception register with severity classification.
* Maker-checker resolution workflow handles all reconciliation breaks: operations staff investigate, compliance officer reviews, and the outcome is recorded in the immutable audit log with supporting evidence.
* Bulk correction capability allows authorised administrators to apply position adjustments with a complete audit trail, CBE-reportable reason codes, and mandatory second-line sign-off.
* Reconciliation dashboards in Arabic and English give NBE's operations team live visibility into outstanding breaks, resolution status, and historical accuracy metrics.

slides.title: Participant Onboarding and KYC Workflow
slides.body: DALP's identity and verification module supports structured participant onboarding with configurable KYC tiers, document collection workflows, approval chains, and ongoing monitoring triggers. Integration with NBE's existing KYC platform ensures no duplication of identity data or compliance processes.

slides.title: Islamic Finance Governance Support
slides.item_0:
Sharia Board Lineage
Approval chain from Sharia board to product configuration is captured in DALP. Product cannot be deployed without documented board sign-off. AAOIFI standard references attached to each instrument record.
slides.item_1:
Sukuk and Islamic Deposit Templates
Pre-configured instrument templates for sukuk, murabaha deposits, wakala structures, and profit-sharing instruments. Compliance rules enforce Sharia restrictions at transaction execution.
slides.item_2:
Profit Distribution Controls
Profit calculation and distribution mechanics are configurable and auditable. Rate-reset events require maker-checker approval. Distribution records are exportable for Sharia audit and FRA reporting.
slides.item_3:
Documentation Controls
All Sharia-related documentation (fatwa, product approval, audit reports) attached to the instrument record in DALP. Immutable timestamping ensures integrity. Arabic-language document support throughout.

slides.title: Deployment Approach
slides.subtitle: Phased Rollout, Testing, Transition, and Training

slides.title: Phased Deployment Approach
slides.body:
* SettleMint's deployment model for NBE is structured in two phases to reduce initial risk, validate controls in a live environment, and build NBE's operational confidence before scaling to full production capacity.
* Phase 0 (Mobilisation, Weeks 1-4) establishes programme governance, design authority, RAID management, and environment provisioning. NBE's architecture, compliance, and operations teams are embedded from day one.
* Phase 1 (Controlled Launch, Months 2-5) delivers core platform configuration, identity and RBAC integration, AML/CFT connector, functional testing, CBE sandbox notification, and go-live with a limited asset scope and participant set.
* Phase 2 (Scaled Operations, Months 6-8) expands to full asset class coverage, Arabic reporting dashboards, complete observability stack, DR certification, and production-scale performance validation.
* At each phase gate, NBE's design authority reviews evidence packs, signs off on acceptance criteria, and formally approves promotion to the next stage. No phase proceeds without explicit gate approval.

slides.title: Phase 1 Controlled Launch Scope
slides.body: Phase 1 delivers core infrastructure with controlled participant set and limited asset scope, validated against CBE requirements before broader rollout.

slides.title: Phase 2 Scaled Operations Scope
slides.body: Phase 2 expands the platform to full production capacity with additional asset classes, Arabic reporting, DR certification, and performance-validated scale.

slides.body: Key milestones from programme initiation through Phase 2 go-live. All dates are indicative pending contract execution and confirmed environment access.

slides.title: Testing and Assurance Programme
slides.item1:
Functional Testing
All asset lifecycle states, compliance rules, RBAC flows, and settlement scenarios tested against NBE's acceptance criteria. Test evidence pack provided to NBE's QA team.
slides.item2:
Non-Functional Testing
Load tests to 10x expected Phase 1 volumes. Latency benchmarks for API and settlement flows. Failover timing measured against RTO/RPO targets.
slides.item3:
Security Testing
Third-party penetration test scoped to NBE environment. OWASP Top 10 coverage. CBE cybersecurity framework compliance review. Results shared with NBE CISO.
slides.item4:
UAT Programme
NBE business, compliance, and operations teams run structured UAT with pre-approved test scripts. Issues tracked in JIRA with SLA-bound resolution by SettleMint.
slides.item5:
Resilience Testing
Controlled DR failover test conducted in UAT environment. Backup restore validated. Incident simulation with full escalation path exercised before go-live.
slides.item6:
Assurance Artefacts
Test summary reports, penetration test findings, UAT sign-off documents, DR test results, and CBE notification packs provided as contractual deliverables.

slides.title: Operational Transition and Runbooks
slides.col1:
Runbook Coverage
* Platform startup, shutdown, and failover procedures
* Incident classification and escalation matrix (P1-P4)
* AML alert investigation and case closure workflow
* Reconciliation break identification and resolution steps
* Maker-checker exception handling for stuck approvals
* CBE evidence request fulfilment procedure
* Arabic-language runbook versions for NBE operations team
slides.col2:
Support Model Handoff
* Hypercare period of 4 weeks post Phase 1 go-live with SettleMint on-site support
* Service desk integration with NBE's ITSM platform (ServiceNow or equivalent)
* Escalation path: NBE L1/L2 to SettleMint L3 with 4-hour P1 response SLA
* Weekly operational review meetings during first 3 months post-launch
* Quarterly service reviews with NBE COO and SettleMint Customer Success
* Knowledge base in Arabic and English maintained by SettleMint throughout contract

slides.title: Training and Knowledge Transfer
slides.col1:
Audience: Operations
* Platform navigation and daily operations workflow
* Reconciliation and exception management
* Incident reporting and escalation procedure
* Arabic-language training materials
* On-site delivery during UAT phase
* Competency assessment included
slides.col2:
Audience: Compliance
* AML alert investigation and case management
* Regulatory evidence extraction and formatting
* Audit log review and export procedures
* CBE and FRA reporting workflow walkthrough
* Policy configuration and limit management
* Sharia governance documentation controls
slides.col3:
Audience: IT and Architecture
* API integration design and testing guide
* Environment management and promotion pipeline
* Observability stack configuration and alerting
* DR procedure and failover execution
* Security controls configuration and review
* SDK and developer tooling onboarding

slides.title: Commercial Overview and TCO
slides.body: SettleMint's commercial model is structured for total-cost-of-ownership clarity across implementation, licensing, support, and operational scaling. All price drivers are disclosed upfront.

slides.title: SettleMint Company and References
slides.body:
* SettleMint was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Brussels, Belgium, with offices in Singapore, the UAE, and India. The company focuses exclusively on enterprise blockchain and digital asset infrastructure for regulated financial institutions.
* SettleMint has deployed production digital asset platforms for central banks, commercial banks, capital markets firms, and payment infrastructure operators across Europe, Asia-Pacific, and the Middle East and Africa region.
* Reference deployments available for NBE's review include regulated tokenized deposit programmes, bond issuance platforms, and Islamic finance instrument infrastructure — all in live institutional environments.
* SettleMint is well-capitalised with strategic institutional investors, no material litigation, and a direct contractual model. NBE will contract directly with SettleMint BV as the primary legal entity.
* Full financial stability documentation, including audited accounts, funding posture, insurance coverage, and key-person risk mitigation, is available in Appendix F of the proposal package.
